docs.title: gogit
docs.text: Generic, dependency-light Git ergonomics for Go, by shelling out to the git CLI. The base layer for higher-level tools — including the bundled gitscan CLI and the OmniDevX telemetry collectors — in the same way gogithub underlies GitHub integrations.

docs.title: Library
docs.text:
• Discovery — Discover(roots, maxDepth) finds repositories under directory roots without descending into them.
  • Commit log — Repo.Log parses commits with calendar-date filtering, author/committer identities, subjects, trailers (Commit.CoAuthors() for Co-authored-by), and --numstat change stats.
  • Metadata — Repo.Branch, Repo.OriginURL, NormalizeRemoteURL (canonical host/path identifiers), Repo.Tags, Repo.TagsWithDates.
repo, _ := gogit.Open("/path/to/repo")
commits, _ := repo.Log(ctx, gogit.LogOptions{
    Since:        weekStart,
    IncludeStats: true,
})

Install:

go get github.com/grokify/gogit

docs.title: gitscan CLI
docs.text:
Scan many repositories for ones needing attention: uncommitted or unpushed changes, replace directives, module/directory mismatches, dependency filters, release ordering, and workflow compliance.

go install github.com/grokify/gogit/cmd/gitscan@latest

See the README for full CLI usage, and Releases for version history.

docs.title: gitscan v0.1.0 Release Notes
docs.text: Release Date: 2026-01-17

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: gitscan is a CLI tool to scan multiple Git repositories and identify repos that need attention. It helps prioritize which repos to update, commit, and push - particularly useful for developers managing many Go projects.

docs.title: Highlights
docs.text:
• CLI tool to scan multiple Git repositories and identify repos needing attention
  • Zero external dependencies - uses only Go standard library

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
go install github.com/grokify/gitscan@v0.1.0

Or build from source:

git clone https://github.com/grokify/gitscan.git
cd gitscan
go build -o gitscan .

docs.title: Repository Analysis
docs.text:
• Uncommitted Changes - Detects modified, added, or deleted files using git status --porcelain
  • Replace Directives - Parses go.mod for replace directives (both single-line and block format), which may indicate local development dependencies
  • Module Name Mismatch - Compares the module name in go.mod with the directory name to identify renamed or copied repos

docs.title: Dependency Search
docs.text:
• Filter repos by dependency with -dep flag to find all repos depending on a specific module
  • Recursive nested go.mod search with -recurse flag for monorepo support

docs.title: User Experience
docs.text:
• Progress bar with percentage, count, and current repo name during scanning
  • Two output formats: human-readable list (default) and compact markdown table
  • Summary statistics showing counts of uncommitted changes, replace directives, and mismatches

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
# Scan all repos in a directory
gitscan ~/go/src/github.com/grokify

# Output as markdown table
gitscan -format table ~/go/src/github.com/grokify

# Find repos depending on a module
gitscan -dep github.com/grokify/mogo ~/go/src/github.com/grokify

# Include nested go.mod files
gitscan -dep github.com/grokify/mogo -recurse ~/go/src/github.com/grokify

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Default Description -dir (required) Directory containing repos to scan -format list Output format: list or table -show-clean false Show repos with no issues -summary true Show summary at the end -dep (none) Filter repos by dependency (module path) -recurse false Recursively search for nested go.mod files

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
• Pre-push audit - Identify repos with uncommitted work before leaving for vacation
  • Dependency cleanup - Find repos with local replace directives that need resolution
  • Repo hygiene - Detect copied/renamed repos with mismatched module names
  • Breaking changes - Find all repos to update before releasing library changes
  • Security patches - Locate repos using vulnerable dependencies

docs.title: License
docs.text: MIT

docs.title: gitscan v0.2.0 Release Notes
docs.text: Release Date: 2026-02-14

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: gitscan v0.2.0 introduces the order subcommand for dependency-aware release ordering, time-based filtering, and significant performance improvements through parallel scanning.

docs.title: Highlights
docs.text:
• New order subcommand for topological dependency ordering
  • Time-based filtering with -since flag
  • Parallel scanning for significantly faster performance

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
go install github.com/grokify/gitscan@v0.2.0

Or build from source:

git clone https://github.com/grokify/gitscan.git
cd gitscan
git checkout v0.2.0
go build -o gitscan .

docs.title: Order Subcommand
docs.text:
The new order subcommand displays repositories in topological dependency order, showing which repos should be updated first based on their internal dependencies.

# Show repos in dependency order
gitscan order ~/go/src/github.com/grokify

# Filter by modification time
gitscan order -s 7d ~/go/src/github.com/grokify

# Include transitive dependents
gitscan order -s 7d -t ~/go/src/github.com/grokify

# Only show repos needing push
gitscan order -s 7d -t -u ~/go/src/github.com/grokify

Output shows dependencies first, then dependents:

Update order (dependencies first):
----------------------------------
  1. mogo                  2026-02-08 12:28
  2. gogithub              2026-02-07 08:09 (depends on: mogo)
  3. goauth                2026-02-09 19:38 (depends on: mogo)
  4. gogoogle              2026-02-09 17:31 (depends on: goauth, mogo)

Total: 4 repos in dependency order

docs.title: Time-Based Filtering
docs.text:
Filter repos by modification time using human-friendly duration formats:

gitscan -s 7d ~/go/src/github.com/grokify   # Last 7 days
gitscan -s 2w ~/go/src/github.com/grokify   # Last 2 weeks
gitscan -s 1m ~/go/src/github.com/grokify   # Last month

docs.title: Transitive Dependency Tracking
docs.text:
With -t flag, include repos that transitively depend on modified repos, even if they weren't directly modified:

gitscan order -s 7d -t ~/go/src/github.com/grokify

docs.title: Unpushed Commit Detection
docs.text:
With -u flag, filter to only show repos with uncommitted changes or unpushed commits:

gitscan order -s 7d -u ~/go/src/github.com/grokify

docs.title: Parallel Scanning
docs.text: Scanning now uses a goroutine worker pool (defaults to GOMAXPROCS) for significantly faster scanning of large directory trees. Expensive operations are performed lazily only when needed.

docs.title: CLI Migration to Cobra
docs.text:
The CLI has been migrated from the standard flag package to Cobra for better UX:

  • Short flags: -d (dir), -f (format), -r (recurse), -s (since)
  • Built-in --help and --version flags
  • Subcommand support (gitscan order)

docs.title: Output Improvements
docs.text:
• Numbered list output with alphabetical sorting
  • Aligned columns for better readability
  • Internal dependency display: (depends on: x, y)

docs.title: Root Command
docs.text: Flag Short Description --since -s Filter repos modified within duration (e.g., 7d, 2w, 1m)

docs.title: Order Subcommand
docs.text: Flag Short Description --transitive -t Include repos that transitively depend on modified repos --unpushed -u Only show repos with uncommitted changes or unpushed commits

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
• Added github.com/spf13/cobra for CLI framework
  • Added github.com/grokify/mogo/fmt/progress for progress bar rendering

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
• Release ordering: Determine correct order to update interdependent modules
  • CI/CD planning: Identify which repos need rebuilding after dependency updates
  • Pre-push audit: Find repos with unpushed work using -u flag
  • Recent activity: Filter to recently modified repos with -s flag

docs.title: License
docs.text: MIT

docs.title: gitscan v0.3.0 Release Notes
docs.text: Release Date: 2026-02-14

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: gitscan v0.3.0 introduces a pluggable git backend architecture with both go-git (pure Go) and git CLI implementations. The git CLI remains the default for optimal performance, while go-git is available as an option for environments without the git binary.

docs.title: Highlights
docs.text:
• Git backend abstraction with go-git and CLI implementations
  • Optional --go-git flag for pure Go git operations (no process spawning)

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
go install github.com/grokify/gitscan@v0.3.0

Or build from source:

git clone https://github.com/grokify/gitscan.git
cd gitscan
git checkout v0.3.0
go build -o gitscan .

docs.title: Git Backend Abstraction
docs.text:
The scanner package now uses a GitBackend interface for pluggable git implementations:

  • GitBackend - Interface with IsRepo() and GetStatus() methods
  • GoGitBackend - Pure Go implementation using go-git library
  • CLIGitBackend - Git CLI implementation (default)

docs.title: go-git Backend Option
docs.text:
Use the --go-git flag to enable pure Go git operations:

# Use go-git library (pure Go, no process spawning)
gitscan --go-git ~/go/src/github.com/grokify

# Works with order subcommand too
gitscan order --go-git ~/go/src/github.com/grokify

This is useful in environments where the git binary is unavailable or when you want to avoid external process spawning.

docs.title: Performance Comparison
docs.text:
Backend Speed (600 repos) Use Case git CLI (default) ~2.5s Most use cases, full compatibility go-git (--go-git) ~10s Environments without git binary

The git CLI backend is recommended for most use cases due to its significantly faster performance.

docs.title: Default Backend
docs.text:
• Git CLI is now the default backend (faster and more compatible)
  • go-git is available as an opt-in alternative via --go-git flag

docs.title: Scanner Package
docs.text:
• New GitBackend interface for pluggable git implementations
  • ScanOptions now includes optional GitBackend field

docs.title: New Flags
docs.text:
Flag Description --go-git Use go-git library instead of git CLI

Available on both scan and order commands.

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text: • Added github.com/go-git/go-git/v5 for pure Go git operations

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
• Containerized environments: Use --go-git when git binary is not installed
  • Embedded applications: Pure Go implementation with no external dependencies
  • Cross-platform builds: Avoid git CLI compatibility issues

docs.title: License
docs.text: MIT

docs.title: gitscan v0.4.0 Release Notes
docs.text: Release Date: 2026-02-21

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: gitscan v0.4.0 restructures the CLI into focused Cobra subcommands, fixing the issue where --since and --dep flags were mutually exclusive but not documented as such. The root command now focuses solely on issue detection, while filtering is handled by dedicated since and dep subcommands. This release also adds Homebrew distribution.

docs.title: Highlights
docs.text:
• Restructured CLI into focused Cobra subcommands
  • New since subcommand with optional --dep flag for AND logic
  • New dep subcommand for dependency filtering
  • Homebrew distribution via brew install gitscan

docs.title: Homebrew (macOS/Linux)
docs.text:
brew tap grokify/tap
brew install gitscan

docs.title: Go Install
docs.text: go install github.com/grokify/gitscan@v0.4.0

docs.title: Build from Source
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/grokify/gitscan.git
cd gitscan
git checkout v0.4.0
go build -o gitscan .

docs.title: Breaking Changes
docs.text:
The --since and --dep flags have been removed from the root command and are now subcommands:

Before (v0.3.0) After (v0.4.0) gitscan --since 7d ~/repos gitscan since 7d ~/repos gitscan --dep github.com/foo/bar ~/repos gitscan dep github.com/foo/bar ~/repos

docs.title: New Command Structure
docs.text:
gitscan [dir]                    # Scan for issues (uncommitted, replace, mismatch)
gitscan since <duration> [dir]   # Filter by modification time
gitscan dep <module> [dir]       # Filter by dependency
gitscan order [dir]              # Show repos in dependency order

docs.title: since Subcommand
docs.text:
Filter repos by modification time:

# Repos modified in last 7 days
gitscan since 7d ~/go/src/github.com/grokify

# Combined with dependency filter (AND logic)
gitscan since 7d --dep github.com/grokify/mogo ~/go/src/github.com/grokify

The --dep flag on the since command applies AND logic - showing only repos that are both recently modified AND depend on the specified module.

docs.title: dep Subcommand
docs.text:
Filter repos by dependency:

# Find all repos depending on a module
gitscan dep github.com/grokify/mogo ~/go/src/github.com/grokify

# Include nested go.mod files
gitscan dep github.com/grokify/mogo -r ~/go/src/github.com/grokify

docs.title: Homebrew Distribution
docs.text:
gitscan is now available via Homebrew:

brew tap grokify/tap
brew install gitscan

Cross-platform binaries are built automatically via GoReleaser for:

  • macOS (amd64, arm64)
  • Linux (amd64, arm64)
  • Windows (amd64, arm64)

docs.title: Code Changes
docs.text:
• New cmd/shared.go with common helpers: resolvePath(), createGitBackend(), parseDuration()
  • New cmd/since.go for the since subcommand
  • New cmd/dep.go for the dep subcommand
  • Simplified cmd/root.go to focus on issue scanning only
  • Added .goreleaser.yaml for release automation
  • Added .github/workflows/release.yaml for GitHub Actions

docs.title: Migration Guide
docs.text:
1. Replace gitscan --since <duration> with gitscan since <duration>
  2. Replace gitscan --dep <module> with gitscan dep <module>
  3. To combine both filters, use: gitscan since <duration> --dep <module>

docs.title: License
docs.text: MIT

docs.title: gogit v0.5.0 Release Notes
docs.text: Release Date: 2026-07-18

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This release renames the repository from gitscan to gogit and reframes it as a generic, dependency-light Git base library for Go — analogous to gogithub for GitHub — with the gitscan CLI preserved unchanged as a bundled tool at cmd/gitscan. The new root package provides repository discovery and commit-log parsing (including Co-authored-by trailer extraction used for AI-attribution analytics in OmniDevX).

docs.title: Breaking Changes
docs.text:
The module path changed:

-import "github.com/grokify/gitscan/scanner"
+import "github.com/grokify/gogit/scanner"

The CLI entrypoint moved from the repository root to cmd/gitscan:

go install github.com/grokify/gogit/cmd/gitscan@latest

GitHub redirects grokify/gitscan URLs to grokify/gogit; update remotes and imports at your convenience.

docs.title: Core git library (new)
docs.text:
• Discover(roots, maxDepth) — depth-bounded repository discovery that does not descend into repositories
  • Repo.Log(ctx, LogOptions) — commit-log parsing with calendar-date filtering (Since/Until), author/committer identities, subjects, trailers, and --numstat change stats
  • Commit.CoAuthors() — Co-authored-by trailer extraction
  • Repo.Branch, Repo.OriginURL, NormalizeRemoteURL — metadata and canonical host/path remote identifiers
  • Repo.Tags, Repo.TagsWithDates — tag listing with creation dates
repo, _ := gogit.Open("/path/to/repo")
commits, _ := repo.Log(ctx, gogit.LogOptions{
    Since:        weekStart,
    IncludeStats: true,
})
for _, c := range commits {
    fmt.Println(c.Hash, c.Author.Email, c.CoAuthors(), c.Insertions)
}

docs.title: Workflow compliance scanning (gitscan CLI)
docs.text: Scan repositories for GitHub workflow compliance against a reference repository (default plexusone/.github) via --check-workflows and --ref-repo.

docs.title: since --unpushed
docs.text: gitscan since <duration> --unpushed filters repositories with uncommitted or unpushed changes (AND logic with existing filters).

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Library:

go get github.com/grokify/gogit@v0.5.0

CLI:

go install github.com/grokify/gogit/cmd/gitscan@v0.5.0

docs.title: Commits
docs.text: See CHANGELOG.md for the full categorized list with commit links.
